mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative earnings outlook produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of June 2017 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following UNRATE declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. This positive earnings outlook produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465,130 million dollars as of June 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following RSAFS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% while inflation remains below 2.5%, signaling restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and increases borrowing costs for corporations. This tightening regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04% and CPIAUCSL at 244.163 as of June 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with below-target inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite restrictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This supply-side contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline while unemployment rises simultaneously, signaling dual demand weakness in both residential investment and consumer confidence. This joint demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% from 3-month average AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,157 thousand units and UNRATE at 4.3% as of June 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of jointly triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons per month, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer income growth. This labor market weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100,000 persons for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146,583 thousand persons as of June 2017
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following payroll deceleration are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus inflation trend) turns positive and rises above 0.5%, signaling monetary policy tightening that increases real borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite. This real tightening produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nominal FEDFUNDS at 1.04% with low inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary tightening with real rate normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when real rates exceed 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while retail sales growth simultaneously slows, signaling broad-based demand weakness across both investment and consumption. This dual demand deceleration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,398.343 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of jointly triggered instances despite dual demand weakness signal.
